session_focus: Verify GUI training data is real and fix write lock to allow any agent on current task.
user_directives: User asked 'is that real training data?' pointing to GUI showing 13.93K tokens, 31 trace artifacts.
User asked 'also is the loop write /autonomoous loop log fixed?'.
User complained: 'we cant create failure paramters just based on who the llm is thats retarded'.
User demanded: 'make it open allowable for any agent to write the single task'.
assistant_responses: Confirmed YES - real training data with instruction/input/output/signal tuples from actual sessions.
Identified lock problem: expired lock file not clearing, same-task continuation blocked.
Fixed security_manager.py acquire_global_write_lock() to allow expired takeover and same-task refresh.
Cleared stale lock file, committed and pushed fix.
training_value: Training data should be verified as real before building UI features on top.
Write locks should be task-scoped, not agent-scoped - any LLM/agent on same task should write.
Expired locks must auto-release to prevent blocking.
Lock failures based on 'which agent' rather than 'which task' create artificial failures.
The single-write constraint should enforce task consistency, not agent identity.
